title: mephitidia retosa
intro: Mephitidia retosa, also known as False Hellebore or Pheasant's Eye, is a perennial plant species within the Ranunculaceae family. It contains protoanemonin, a toxic compound that has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Mephitidia retosa has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, potentially benefiting respiratory issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tincture or poultice, which can be applied topically to alleviate symptoms of wounds, eczema, and other skin irritations.
